May 22 D1 candle: O=29,476 H=29,664 L=29,424 C=29,482 — tiny body (5pts, 2.2%), large upper wick (182pts) indicating strong rejection at ATH. No SMT divergence: both NAS100 and ES made highs together, confirming trend but showing no reversal signal. OHLC: price closed near open, well below daily high. Sellers defended 29,600+ area. Key observation: NQ futures swept 29,995 in electronic session (above cash ATH of 29,679) — potential BSL sweep/Judas move.

reasoning: NAS100 is at a critical inflection point. D1 structure is bullish (HH/HL from Apr 28 low) but price is in deep Premium (93% of dealing range) at/near ATH. The May 22 D1 candle shows rejection — large upper wick (182pts) with tiny body (5pts) — sellers defended the 29,600+ area aggressively. NQ futures swept to 29,995 in electronic session (above cash ATH), which could be a BSL sweep/Judas manipulation before reversal, or genuine breakout continuation. SMT shows NO divergence — both NAS100 and ES made highs together, which confirms the trend but removes the reversal catalyst. ORB score of 42/100 reflects: (1) HTF bias bullish but exhausted at ATH (18/25), (2) no VWAP data (5/15), (3) volume below average at 46% of norm (8/20), (4) D1 rejection candle reduces timing score (6/15), (5) no SMT divergence + no M15 confirmation (5/15), (6) VIX at 16.70 shows low vol but compressed (5/10). Memorial Day holiday means thin liquidity yesterday — today's cash open (09:30 ET) will set the tone. The electronic session BSL sweep at 29,995 sets up a potential Classic Tuesday High scenario IF price sweeps higher during London/early NY then reverses. NO ENTRY until Kill Zone active and M15 structure confirms. Bias is neutral-bearish because: deep Premium + rejection candle + BSL sweep = conditions favor short setup IF price shows weakness during Kill Zone. If price breaks and holds above 29,679 with volume, bias flips to bullish breakout.
